en.value: Provide local capacity building support and workforce training opportunities through recruitment of local youth and providing training to build relevant construction and reconstruction skills within the communities. An on-the-job approach will also be used so that contextual knowledge, first-hand observation, and skills are developed through practice.Identification of the immediate needs related to education response and recovery.Engagement with respective LEUs, municipal engineers, SMCs and school communities to enhance their planning, budgeting, executing, and monitoring capacities.Assisting the LEUs and SMCs to plan for a transitional phase while they wait for a permanent building, including preparation of a basic layout of the location of all temporary, transitional, and existing school blocks.Providing orientation to LEUs, SMCs, and teachers on the MOEST’s Comprehensive School Safety (CSS) Master Plan, the CSS Minimum package, and CSS Implementation Guidelines.Supporting the schools to prepare their disaster preparedness plans, and incorporate these elements of CSS related training and orientations in respective School Improvement Plans (SIPs).Construction of accessible transitional learning centers (Type-2 TLCs), two-room toilets, and hand-washing stations.Providing training to the members of PTAs, SMCs, and teachers on proper maintenance of TLCs.Distribution of GON-approved, standardized packages of teaching and learning materials: story books, letter and word charts, number charts, wall calendars, notebooks, pens, and other educational wall displays.

executing_agency: Ministry of Education, Science and Technology

version_summary.change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API
